regions.name: Galápagos Islands (Santa Cruz base)
regions.stay_type: mid-range lodge in Puerto Ayora with day-trip access

activities.category: Tortoise reserve and Charles Darwin Research Station
Snorkeling with sea lions and marine iguanas
Island day trip by speedboat to Española or Bartolomé

common_mistakes: Treating the Galápagos as a quick add-on — three nights minimum for Santa Cruz-based visits, five for a liveaboard that actually covers multiple islands
Visiting Otavalo market on any day other than Saturday — the midweek market is a fraction of the real thing
Flying into the Amazon without malaria prevention discussion with your doctor ahead of time
Booking a Galápagos liveaboard without checking the route map — 'Galápagos cruise' covers everything from day-boats to proper multi-day vessels with very different wildlife access

regions.name: Quito
regions.stay_type: restored colonial hotel in the historic center

activities.category: Old Town UNESCO walking route
Teleferico cable car to Pichincha volcano flank

regions.name: Avenue of the Volcanoes
regions.stay_type: hacienda with views of multiple volcano peaks

activities.category: Cotopaxi National Park acclimatization hike
Quilotoa crater lake loop hike
Andean village market and weaving workshop
Ruta de las Cascadas cycling descent
Tungurahua volcano viewpoint at dusk
White-water rafting on the Pastaza

common_mistakes: Attempting Cotopaxi's upper slopes without proper acclimatization — altitude sickness is real and dangerous above 4,500m
Driving the Avenue of the Volcanoes in a rush — the haciendas along this route deserve at least two nights each to settle in
Visiting Baños during Carnival when the town becomes extremely crowded and rafting conditions are unpredictable
